center.name: 亞馬遜雨林
center.summary: 亞馬遜雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联52种鸟类，包括白喉䳍、巴西穴䳍、巴氏穴䳍等52种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Amazonian black-tyrant (Knipolegus poecilocercus) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is particularly native to riverbanks of the Amazon rainforest

birds.evidence: The Amazonian pygmy owl occurs in the central and southwestern areas of the Amazonia region

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-girdled barbet is found in southern Amazonia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Amazonia it is found in several forest types including transitional and swamp forest, gallery forest, and terra firme and várzea forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in forests (though generally avoids extensive humid forests such as the Amazon), woodland, savanna and palm groves.Juniper, T., & M

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the southeastern Amazon rainforest in Brazil and Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The dusky-billed parrotlet is found in the Amazon rainforest in South America, where it is locally fairly common; it also occurs in the Andes and the Amazonian foothills, the Amazon River outlet, and Marajo Island

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The fulvous-chinned nunlet is found in southwestern Amazonia south of the Amazon River, from eastern Peru and northern Bolivia east in southwestern Brazil as far as the Madeira River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They are found in two separate general populations, one in the northern Amazon rainforest and the other in the Cerrado

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in tropical humid forests in South America, where its range includes the Amazon, the Guiana Shield, Trinidad, and the Atlantic Forest in eastern Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Guianan woodcreeper is found in northern Amazonia in the eastern Venezuelan states of Delta Amacuro and Bolívar, the Guianas, and northern Brazil north of the Amazon River from the Rio Negro east to the Atlantic ocean

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Amazonia from Ecuador and Peru east through southern Colombia and Venezuela to the Guianas, in far northern Bolivia, and in much of western Brazil. The long-tailed potoo inhabits the interiors of lowland tropical evergreen forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the Amazon rainforest in South America, from the lowlands into the foothills of the Andes.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ |title=IOC World Bird List (v 11.2) |last1=Gill |first1= F

birds.evidence: It is found in the Amazon and Atlantic Forest of South America

birds.evidence: It is the only member of its genus that is found in the lowlands of the Amazon rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the western Amazon rainforest of Bolivia, Brazil, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the western Amazon rainforest of South America; its range extends from southern Colombia south through eastern Ecuador and Peru and east through extreme northwestern Bolivia and into western Amazonian Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The razor-billed curassow is found in much of Amazonia, mostly south of the Amazon River in eastern Peru, northern Bolivia, and northern Brazil all the way to the Atlantic coast

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rufous-necked puffbird is found in southern Amazonia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rufous potoo is found in Amazonia from Venezuela south to Peru and east into Brazil, French Guiana, and Guyana

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in wooded habitats in the highlands of the northern and central Andes and the Venezuelan Coastal Range, and in lowlands of the western Amazon

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Venezuela it is usually found in areas of sandy soil; in southeastern Brazil's Paraná state it occurs in gallery forest, and in upper Amazonia it shuns igapó forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The southerly ones are thought to be migratory, spending the austral winter in southern Amazonia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The semicollared puffbird is found in western Amazonia where far western Brazil, southeastern Peru, and far northern Bolivia meet

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Amazon rainforest in Brazil, Bolivia, Peru, Ecuador, Colombia, Venezuela, and French Guiana

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Most of its range is the Amazon in South America, but it is also found in forests of the Chocó in Ecuador and Colombia, and southern Central America from Panama to Honduras

birds.evidence: marginatus, throughout Amazonia, from Venezuela, Ecuador, Colombia, Peru, and Bolivia to near the mouth of the river in Brazil The southern nightingale-wren inhabits the lower levels of dense humid tropical forest

birds.evidence: It is found in the canopy of the Amazon rainforest in South America

birds.evidence: This species is found across the Amazonian regions of Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru

birds.evidence: probatus of the Tapajos scythebill is found in southwestern Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon River between the Madeira and Tapajós rivers

birds.evidence: It is native to Amazonia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: turdinus hypostictus is found in the southern and western Amazon rainforest in south-eastern Colombia, eastern Ecuador, eastern Peru, northern Bolivia and Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Keeney|doi=10.2173/bow.whttit2.01 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/whttit2/1.0/introduction}} | synonyms = | range_map = Tityra leucura map.svg }} The white-tailed tityra (Tityra leucura) is a medium-sized passerine bird about 19 cm long with pied plumage, mostly white or very pale grey, but with a black crown and wings. It is found in a small area of the Amazon rainforest near the Madeira River in the far west of Brazil close to the Peruvian border

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Xingu scythebill is found in eastern Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon between the Xingu and Tocantins rivers and south into Mato Grosso state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the Amazon rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: eriphile, southeastern Brazil from Bahia south into Paraguay and northeastern Argentina's Misiones Province The fork-tailed woodnymph inhabits a variety of landscapes within Amazonia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: savana, is found in central and southern Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Argentina (south to the Río Negro), and overwinters in Amazonia, a large portion of northern South America (i.e., within the Orinoco River Basin), and Trinidad and Tobago, occasionally appearing in the West Indies.{{Cite journal|last=Rosenberg|first=Gary H.|date=July 2010|title=Field Guide to the Songbirds of South America.— Robert S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nocturnal curassow is found in middle and upper Amazonia, from far southern Venezuela, southeastern Colombia, eastern Ecuador, and northeastern Peru into western and central Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is further found from coastal Maranhão southwest to southwestern Brazil, Bolivia, and Pampas de Heath in far southeastern Peru, and south through Piauí and Bahia to northern Argentina and Uruguay. It was previously thought to have gone extinct in Tucumán in northwestern Argentina by the late 1990s, but was rediscovered there in the early 2010s. The species's range in the Amazon rainforest may be increasing due to deforestation. Similarly, it has only been recorded from Uruguay recently; previously, the southward limit of its range was Lagoa dos Patos in Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The screaming piha has a disjunct distribution : widespread thoughout the Amazon rainforest, as well as the Atlantic Forest (Pernambuco and Bahia coastal forests). The screaming piha inhabits lowland evergreen forest, primarily terra firme but also locally igapó and várzea flooded forest. In elevation it ranges from sea level to in Brazil. It reaches in Venezuela though most records there are below .{{cite book | last =Hilty | first =Steven L

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is native to western Amazonian Brazil, northern Bolivia, and eastern Peru, in South America. It is also found in eastern Ecuador. Over its range, it is considered uncommon

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is found in South America south of the Amazon rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: (2008) | range_map = Crypturellus strigulosus map.svg }} The Brazilian tinamou (Crypturellus strigulosus) is a type of tinamou found in tropical moist lowland forest in regions of Amazonian South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In the lowlands of Amazonia it also occurs in várzea and in some areas pure stands of Guadua bamboo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in a wide range of open to semiopen habitats, but generally avoids the interior of dense forest (such as the Amazon)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: 2| page = 68| year = 2006| publisher = Christopher Helm | isbn=0-7136-7243-9}} It primarily inhabits undisturbed tropical lowland forests as well as savannas and grasslands with these forests nearby. It is often seen near swamps or marshy places in the forests. This bird is often the most numerous or only vulture present in primary lowland forests in its range, but in the Amazon rainforest, it is typically outnumbered by the greater yellow-headed vulture, while typically outnumbered by the lesser yellow-headed, turkey, and American black vulture in more open habitats.{{cite book | last1 = Restall| first1 = Robin| last2 = Rodner| first2 = Clemencia| last3 = Lentino| first3 = Miguel| title=Birds of Northern South America: An Identification Guide

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: (2008) | range_map = Tinamus guttatus map.svg }} The white-throated tinamou (Tinamus guttatus) is a species of bird native to the Amazon rainforest of Brazil, northern Bolivia, southeastern Colombia, northeastern Ecuador, eastern Peru and southern Venezuela

birds.evidence: The white-bearded hermit is found in Amazonia, from the Andean foothills of western and southern Venezuela south through eastern Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru to northeastern Bolivia, and east throughout western Amazonian Brazil

birds.evidence: The fiery-tailed awlbill is found in Amazonia, from southeastern Venezuela east through the Guianas into northern Brazil and south into Brazil as far west as Acre and as far east as Maranhão and Tocantins
